rationale.R1.2: The agent identified four internal contradictions/non-sequiturs in the argument chain, including the 'fully unsupervised' claim and the AUC-to-prevalence leap.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is not explicitly addressed in the text, but issue 6 (feature/label leakage) is a hypothesis based on conventional definitions of refusals/degeneration.
rationale.RX.3: The deployment scope paragraph asserts that ROUTERSCAN can be deployed on any router's logs without further labeling effort, but this claim conflates 'the model produces a score' with 'the score is valid on a new distribution'.
rationale.RX.4: The Results paragraph reports AUC 0.91 and F1 0.74 at the validation-tuned operating point, but issue 6 (feature/label leakage) may be partially tautological.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are not explicitly addressed in the text, but the agent recommends targeted rewrites to clarify the chain-of-reasoning gap.
